rules: AVOID: Two consecutive <hr /> tags appear at the start of post body (detected 2x in published posts)
AVOID: Post ends with incomplete sentence 'I'm not' — text is cut off mid-paragraph (detected 2x in published posts)
AVOID: Phrase 'slow burn' is used in context of supplement effectiveness—common AI filler (detected 2x in published posts)
AVOID: sec5 states 'probiotics don't work effectively' but entire post recommends taking them — direct cont (detected 2x in published posts)
AVOID: Phrase 'it's worth noting' or 'it's not worth' pattern appears throughout — common AI filler (detected 2x in published posts)
AVOID: Title containing 'Complete', 'Guide', 'Dosage', or 'Benefits' — strong AI template signal, kills authenticity
AVOID: Duplicate nutrient name in title (e.g., 'Probiotics Complete: Probiotics : Guide...') — indicates broken template
AVOID: Image alt text like 'A bottle of [X] and Complete on a wooden kitchen counter' — generic template, use natural descriptions like 'my probiotic bottle next to breakfast'
AVOID: Key Takeaways that say 'take with fat for best absorption' for water-soluble or non-fat-soluble supplements (probiotics, B vitamins, etc.) — factually wrong and template-obvious
AVOID: friend/Sarah/coworker/colleague pattern — having a named friend appear is now a repeated pattern across posts, readers notice
AVOID: Success story arc that is too clean: Problem → Week 4 → Improvement → Routine → Success — must include genuine doubt, setback, or uncertainty ('maybe placebo', 'not sure', 'I almost quit')
AVOID: Repeating the same core message (take with food, bloating improved, don't quit, week 4) across 3+ sections — information density drops, post feels padded
AVOID: FAQ section in every single post — occasional posts without FAQ feel more authentic and varied
AVOID: Image alt text 'probiotics complete guide why i almost didn't try it' reads like a URL slug (all low (detected 2x in published posts)
AVOID: sec5 final paragraph: 'There's one detail I didn't mention yet — and it's the part that changed my r (detected 2x in published posts)
AVOID: Title containing 'Worth Taking' or 'What the Research Says' — sounds like a product review template, not a personal experience post
AVOID: Template substitution failure '[Nutrient] And Complete' or '[Nutrient] and Complete' in body text — indicates broken placeholder, must be caught before publish
AVOID: Caption like 'Personal observations on [Nutrient] And Complete' — AI template filler, use specific scene descriptions like 'The bottle I almost stopped taking during week 2.'
AVOID: Phrase 'I wasn't just surviving—I was thriving' — overused AI climax line, use understated language like 'I felt more stable'
AVOID: Contradicting statements about the same trigger within one post (e.g., coffee is fine in section 1, coffee causes nausea in section 4) — pick one position and stay consistent throughout
AVOID: Image alt text is all lowercase with hyphens, appears auto-generated slug: ' month one: where things (detected 2x in published posts)

rules.rule: EDITOR-R1: After substitution, check for duplicate phrases (e.g., 'phrase phrase') — run_editor_checks() catches this.
EDITOR-R2: After substitution, check for broken sentences (word.word) — no space after period.
EDITOR-R3: H1, OG title, JSON-LD headline must be identical after any title change.
EDITOR-R4: OG description and JSON-LD description must be identical.
EDITOR-R6: Exaggeration (everything changed, life-changing) requires evidence paragraph.
EDITOR-R7: Reader perspective check — must answer: story, what changed, mistake, title fulfilled.
AVOID: applying a single 60% experience ratio rule to all post types. comprehensive_guide → 45~55% | experience/longtail → 60~75%

rules: AVOID: Sentence fragment: 'After I started the stack—and I'm talking the real stack, both at once' ends abr (detected 2x in published posts)

rules.rule: AVOID: Definitive health claim 'That's basically useless' — replace with personal hedge: 'didn't seem to work well for me'
AVOID: Definitive health claim 'makes you stronger' / 'helps you recover faster' — replace with 'seemed to help with strength/recovery' or 'for me at least'
AVOID: Mechanistic claim 'absorption depends on X' — replace with 'may absorb better when...' or 'from what I've read'
AVOID: Blanket dismissal 'X does nothing' — replace with 'X likely does very little for me' or 'I didn't notice much difference'
WRITER-RULE: 모든 문장을 쓸 때 '이게 1인칭 경험 문장인가?' 확인. 아니면 경험형으로 재작성.
WRITER-RULE: 건강 효과 주장 시 반드시 'seemed to', 'for me', 'I felt like', 'may', 'I probably' 사용.
CRITIC-RULE: 발행 전 경험담 비율 60% 미만이면 REJECT. Writer에게 조언→경험 전환 요구.
CRITIC-RULE: og:description에 'the research', 'studies show', 'X is known to' 등 3인칭 연구 언급 금지. 개인 경험형만 허용.

WRITER/CRITIC-RULE: 경험담 비율 타입별 기준 (Rule5 v8.9) — comprehensive_guide:45~55% | how_i_use/personal_guide:60~75% | experiment_log:60~70% | wrong_culprit:65~80% | unexpected_tradeoff:65~80% | regret_ignoring:65~80% | 기타:60~75%

rules.rule: TITLE-YMYL: Healed/Cured/Fixed/Eliminated/Disappeared 금지 — helped with/started working/became less noticeable로 대체
BODY-YMYL: crashes disappeared/fog lifted/pain gone 금지 — became less noticeable/felt clearer/seemed to improve로 대체
BODY-GENERALIZATION: you can feel/neither one is/everyone who... 금지 — for me/seemed to/may/in some situations로 대체

rules: AVOID: DOCTYPE, <html>, <head>, and <style> tags are present in the post body. These should only appear in  (detected 2x in published posts)
AVOID: The post describes 7+ different body systems/symptoms as 'improved': respiratory clarity, mental cla (detected 2x in published posts)
AVOID: Timeline conflicts exist: 'Week 6: Something Actually Shifted' section states 'By week 4' changes we (detected 2x in published posts)
AVOID: Post title is 'Nac Complete: Nac : Guide to Dosage and Benefits' (guidebook style) but og:title and  (detected 2x in published posts)
AVOID: og:description and meta description state 'The research on Vitamin C is often different from real-wo (detected 2x in published posts)
AVOID: og:description starting with research language. 'Research on', 'What the research says', 'Studies show', '[Nutrient] supplementation:' are all BANNED in og:description.
AVOID: H1 pattern 'Is X Worth Taking? What the Research Says' — use experience-based title instead.
AVOID: Assertive health claims — 'made a measurable difference', 'proved significantly more effective', 'the answer was always X'. Use hedged personal experience language.
AVOID: 'made a huge difference' → use 'felt noticeably different over time'
AVOID: 'I can see the results' → use 'I can notice the difference in my routine'
AVOID: 'energy levels are stable' → use 'energy levels feel more stable' (hedge with feel/seem)
SYSTEM: hub_page_generator.py uses logging.info() not sys.stdout.write() — stdout is closed in orchestrator context.
SYSTEM: Hub auto-update runs after every publish via add_post_to_hub(). Categories: minerals/vitamins/performance/sleep_stress/gut_metabolism/longevity_antioxidants/cognitive_mood.
